original: Battle of Harlaw, one of the bloodiest battles in Scotland, takes place
Citizens of Leeuwarden, Netherlands, rebel against ban on foreign beer
Second attempt to circumnavigate the globe as seven ships departs Corunna headed by García Jofre de Loaísa on orders of King Charles I of Spain for the Spice Islands (only one will make it)
Spanish army and German mercenaries conquer Namur
Treason of Don Juan in Brussels
Anthony Johnson, a free African American, receives grant of 250 acres in Virginia
Great Fire of 1660 in Constantinople: two-thirds of the city is destroyed, including 280,000 wooden houses, with a death toll of around 40,000
First settlers from Germany to the US leave aboard the Concord
Expedition to establish French colony in Louisiana leaves La Rochelle with four ships led by René-Robert Cavelier, Sieur de La Salle (the expedition is disastrous with only 15 survivors)
French trader and soldier Antoine de la Mothe Cadillac founds trading post at Fort Pontchartrain; later becomes the city of Detroit
English and Dutch troops occupy Gibraltar
Battle of Denain: France under Villars defeats the Dutch army
Georgia becomes a protectorate of Tsarist Russia
France passes first copyright law
Slavery is abolished in Chile
Benjamin Bonneville leads the first wagon train across the Rocky Mountains through Wyoming's South Pass
HMS Beagle departs Maldonado, Uruguay
Rotary-type printing press patents received by Richard March Hoe, NYC
Olympics defeat St. George 25-17 at St. George's Cricket Grounds in Philadelphia in the first baseball game played in an enclosed field
Battle of Battle Mountain, Virginia
Tennessee becomes the first Confederate state readmitted to the Union
First trans-US rail service begins
First time federal troops are used to combat strikers
First commercial hydroelectric power plant in the world begins generating electricity in Grand Rapids, Michigan
Arabi Pasha declares a holy war in Egypt
China takes British protectorate of Burma
For the only time in the history of the US Tennis Championships, an event is held off the Eastern Seaboard: the Men's Doubles Championship in Chicago
Race riot in New Orleans kills two white policemen
In Turkey, the Sultan Abdul-Hamid, under pressure from within the Ottoman Empire, appoints a commission to consider reforms that might pacify Macedonian revolutionaries
After days of discussion with his ministers, Sultan Abdul Hamid of Turkey announces he is restoring the liberal constitution of 1876 and will become more responsive to demands of dissidents
American Johnny Hayes wins the London Olympic marathon in a Games record time of 2:55:18.4 after Dorando Pietri of Italy is disqualified for receiving assistance before the finish line
Brooklyn Superbas pitcher Nap Rucker strikes out 16 Pittsburgh Pirates in a 1-0 victory at Washington Park, Brooklyn
Matador Juan Belmonte (18) kills his first bull
Excursion ship Eastland capsizes in Lake Michigan, killing 852 people
Race riot in Washington, D.C., kills 6 and injures 100
Treaty of Lausanne signed by Allied Powers and Turkey recognizes modern boundaries of Turkey and British control of Cyprus
The Menin Gate war memorial is unveiled at Ypres
New York to San Francisco foot race ends after 2½ months; the winner is 60-year-old Monteverde
A fire at a home for the elderly in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, kills 48 people
George Gunn scores 183, and his son, George Vernon, scores 100* in the same innings
First ptarmigan hatched and reared in captivity in Ithaca, New York
First greetings telegram sent in Britain
The world's first children's railway opens in Tbilisi, USSR
118°F (48°C) recorded in Minden, Nebraska (state record)
121°F (49°C) recorded near Alton, Kansas (state record)
Generals Mola and Cabanellas form Spanish anti-government
Alabama drops charges against five Blacks accused of rape in Scottsboro
First illegal "Newsletter of Pieter It Hen" is published in Netherlands

Louis Einthoven, Linthorst Homan, and Jan de Quay found the Nederlandsche Unie (Dutch Union), an organization that seeks to cooperate with Nazi German occupiers without abandoning Dutch sovereignty and culture
Nazis kill entire Jewish population of Grodz, Lithuania
German troops occupy Rostov-on-Don in Southern Russia for the second time
Transport #10 departs Drancy, France, with 1,000 French Jews sent to Auschwitz concentration camp; five survive until the end of the war
Operation Gomorrah: RAF begins bombing Hamburg (until 3rd August), creating a firestorm and killing 42,600 people
300 Allied bombers drop firebombs on German positions at Saint-Lô, France
Soviet forces liberate concentration camp Majdanek
US troops land on Tinian
WWII: A suicide attack by a Japanese Kaiten manned torpedo sinks the US Navy destroyer USS Underhill west of Guam; nearly half of the 236 crew members are killed
Nine Spokane baseball players (Western League) die in a bus crash
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Bikini Atoll
Four baseball players with the Duluth, Minnesota Dukes (St. Louis Cardinals Class C farm team) die in a crash
Marvin the Martian, Warner Bros. cartoon character created by Chuck Jones and Michael Maltese (Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series), first debuts in "Haredevil Hare"
V-2/WAC Corporal rocket launch; first launch from Cape Canaveral
112°F (44°C) recorded in Louisville, Georgia (state record)
Emile Zatopek runs Olympic record in the 5,000 meters (14:06.6)
US President Harry Truman settles 53-day steel strike
KEYT TV channel 3 in Santa Barbara, California (ABC) begins broadcasting
Ali Sastroamidjojo resigns from the government in Indonesia
Dodgers lose to the Reds 2-1 at Jersey City's Roosevelt Stadium
KTVC TV channel 6 in Ensign, KS (CBS) begins broadcasting
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Fourteen people are named as the first life peers in the UK
Test cricket debuts against New Zealand for Dexter, Illingworth, and Subba Row
500,000th Dutch TV set registered
A US commercial plane is hijacked to Cuba
Edwin Newman becomes news anchor of NBC's "Today Show"
124 Unification Church couples wed in Korea
Dutch government of Marijnen forms
Race riot in Rochester, New York, kills 4
British rock group "The Animals" appear for the first time in the British charts
Chinese army, air force, and fleet repress uprising in Wuhan City
Norway requests European Economic Community membership
Race riots in Cambridge, Maryland
Race riots in Detroit force postponement of Tigers-Orioles game
The Beatles sign a petition in The Times newspaper to legalize marijuana
International Lawn Tennis Association institutes 9-point tiebreak rule
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
WUHQ TV channel 41 in Battle Creek, Michigan (ABC) begins broadcasting
Bugojno group is caught by Yugoslav security forces
Jigme Singye Wangchuk becomes King of Bhutan at 16
US Supreme Court unanimously rules Nixon to turn over Watergate tapes
John Naber is the first to swim 200 m backstroke in under 2 minutes
Seattle's John Montague pitches 6 2/3 innings of perfect relief, tying the two-game record of retiring 33 consecutive batsmen
MLB Cincinnati RedsPete Roseties NL hitting streak of 37 games
Mohammed Ali Rajai is elected president of Iran
Heavy rain causes a mudslide that destroys a bridge at Nagasaki, Japan, killing 299
KHJ (LA) and KFRC (San Francisco) become second and third stereo AM stations
Single "Eye of the Tiger" by Survivor from "Rocky III" soundtrack starts a 6-week run at #1 on US charts (Grammy for Best Rock Performance)
Sonya Robinson (Milwaukee), 23, crowned 16th Miss Black America
13th Commonwealth Games open in Edinburgh, Scotland
SF Federal jury convicts navy radioman Jerry Whitworth of espionage
IBM-PC DOS Version 3.3 (updated) is released
USSR performs underground nuclear test
US and Jamaica play a scoreless tie in the second round of the 1990 World Cup
Paula Gwynn, 22, is crowned the 21st Miss Black America
Ms. Magazine hits newsstands again after an 8-month hiatus
US warships in the Persian Gulf are placed on alert after Iraq masses nearly 30,000 troops near its border with Kuwait
A team of international astronomers led by Georgina Dransfield from the University of Birmingham announces the discovery of a planet outside the solar system
Ottawa Rough Riders Board of Directors resigns
Mandala airlines plane crashes on Ambon island, eastern Indonesia, killing 70
Shankar Dayal Sharma is elected ninth Indian President
New York MetAnthony Youngloses a record 27th consecutive game
Vince Colemaninjures three when he throws a cherry bomb at Dodger fans
Asociación de Estados del Caribe (AEC) forms
Bodo kills 37 Muslims in Bashbari NE India
Russell Eugene Weston Jr. bursts into the United States Capitol and opens fire killing two police officers. He is later ruled to be incompetent to stand trial.
An Iranian warship in the Caspian Sea threatens a BP oil exploration ship off the coast of Azerbaijan

Simeon Saxe-Coburg-Gotha, the last Tsar of Bulgaria when he was a child, is sworn in as Prime Minister of Bulgaria, becoming the first monarch in history to regain political power through a democratic election to a different office
James Traficant is expelled from the United States House of Representatives on a vote of 420 to 1
Libya frees all six of the medics in the HIV trial in Libya

FINA Congress votes to ban all body-length swimsuits; men's suits to cover from the waist to the knee, and women's from shoulder to knee; fabric must be a "textile" or woven material; effective 1 January 2010
Four barrels containing 248 human fetuses are found in Sverdlovsk, Russia
John Dramani becomes President of Ghana after the death of President John Atta Mills
A conflict between the Knights Templar Cartel and Mexican police in Michoacán leaves 22 people dead
A high-speed train derails in Santiago de Compostela, Spain, killing 80 people and injuring 140
Air Algérie Flight 5017 crashes in Mali, killing 116 people
Fuad Masum is elected as the president of Iraq
Over 10,000 Palestinians protest Israel's operation in Gaza; two Palestinians killed after Al-Aqsa Brigades members fire at Israeli forces
Reuven Rivlin is sworn in as the president of Israel
Polish President Andrzej Duda vetoes two laws to put Polish courts under political control
Taliban suicide bomber on a motorbike kills at least 26 and injures 50 in Lahore, Pakistan
Taliban suicide bus bombing in Kabul kills at least 38, mainly employees of the Afghan Ministry of Mines and Petroleum
First bison born in Banff National Park, Alberta, Canada, in 140 years after being reintroduced
Wildfires near Athens, Greece, kill 91 and injure 104, with over 600 rescued from the coast by boats
Facebook agrees to pay a $5 billion fine, the largest ever for violating consumer privacy, to the US Federal Trade Commission
Global warming is the fastest in 2,000 years and scientific consensus that humans are the cause is at 99%, according to three major reports published in journals "Nature" and "Nature Geoscience"

Second heatwave of the summer in Western Europe sets record temperatures in Belgium at Kleine Brogel 39.9°C (102°F), in the Netherlands at Eindhoven 39.3°C (102°F), and in Germany at Geilenkirchen 40.5°C (104°F)
Ten millionth Mini car produced during its 60th anniversary year in Oxford, England
Évian Championship Women's Golf, Évian Resort GC: Brooke Henderson of Canada edges American rookie Sophia Schubert by one stroke to win her second major title
Heatwave across America's Northeast with many cities, including Boston, St. Louis, and Philadelphia, declaring heat emergencies
Over 1,100 refugees rescued in separate operations across the Mediterranean in a few days by coast guards and charities, with five bodies recovered
Wild monkey attacks at least 42 people, including in their homes, in Yamaguchi City, Japan, leading police to be armed with tranquilizer guns
Dengue outbreak in Bangladesh, caused by heavy monsoon rains, claims more than 170 lives, with 33,000 hospitalizations, according to local health officials
Gender pay gap in America drops to its lowest level with women earning 84 cents for every $1 men earn full-time (up from 78 cents a decade ago)

Israel’s parliament passes a controversial “reasonableness” bill, taking away the ability of the Supreme Court to declare government decisions unreasonable, as the first bill in a plan to weaken the judiciary amid large, widespread protests
Ugandan-English runner Deo Kato (36) begins trek from the Long March to Freedom monument in Cape Town, South Africa, to London, England to highlight the story of human migration
US Justice department sues Texas over a floating barrier installed in the Rio Grande river between the US and Mexico to deter migrants, citing humanitarian concerns
Wildfires across Algeria kill at least 34 people amid temperatures of 48°C (118°F)
"Inside Out 2" surpasses "Frozen II" to become the highest-grossing animated film in history, making $1.46 billion worldwide
Meta announces it has removed 63,000 Instagram accounts tied to sextortion schemes associated with Nigerian Yahoo Boys criminal group
Philippine officials declare a “state of calamity” around Manila as Typhoon Gaemi/Carina causes massive flooding, displacing 600,000 people and sinking an oil tanker
Taiwan declares a Typhoon Day, closing all schools and businesses as Typhoon Gaemi makes landfall in eastern Taiwan with winds of 240 kmh (150 mph)
The Park Fire is started by an arsonist in Butte County, California, and later grows to become the seventh-largest in state history
Wildfire destroys much of the historic Canadian town of Jasper, Alberta, inside Jasper National Park
Battle of Nocera between Ranulf II of Alife andRoger IIof Sicily
The Crusaders led by Louis VII of France and Conrad III of Germany lay siege to Damascus during theSecond Crusade(abandoned 28 July)
Wars of the Roses: Battle of Edgecote Moor pits the forces of Richard Neville, 16th Earl of Warwick, against those of KingEdward IV
French explorerJacques Cartierclaims for France the lands around Gaspé by erecting a 30-foot cross at Pointe-Penouille
Mary, Queen of Scots, is forced to abdicate; her 1-year-old son becomes KingJames VIof Scotland
Edmund Halleyenters The Queen's College, Oxford, as an undergraduate
George Washingtonis elected to the Virginia House of Burgesses, representing Frederick County
William Clarkis willed the enslaved man York, who goes on to accompany him and play an important role in theLewis and ClarkExpedition
Harrisburg Pennsylvanian newspaper publishes results of the first public opinion poll, showing a clear lead forAndrew Jackson
Brigham Youngand his Mormon followers arrive at Salt Lake City, Utah
TsarNicholas II(Russia) and EmperorWilhelm II(Germany) sign the Björkö Treaty, whereby each country agrees to come to the other's defense if attacked by European powers
Trial of Dutch exotic dancerMata Haribegins in Paris for allegedly spying for Germany and thus causing the deaths of 50,000 soldiers
US PresidentHerbert HooverproclaimsKellogg-BriandPact which renounces war
Paavo Nurmiruns a world record in the 2 mile (8:59.6)
German judge Vogt signs deed of accusation againstMarinus van der Lubbefor setting the Reichstag fire
FDRdemands Japanese troops out of Indochina
American Western film "High Noon" is released, directed by Fred Zinnemann and starringGary Cooper,Grace Kellyand Thomas Mitchell
Brendan Behan's first play "Quare Fellow" premieres in London
Ted Williamsis fined $250 for spitting at Boston fans again
Casey Stengelresigns as manager of NY Mets
Charles de Gaullesays "Vive le Quebec libre! Long live free Quebec!"
First modern hospice, St. Christopher's, is founded by Dr.Cicely Saundersin London, beginning modern palliative care and the hospice movement
Hoyt Wilhelmpitches in a record 907th major league game breakingCy Young's record for pitching appearances
"Death Wish," based on the novel by Brian Garfield, directed by Michael Winner, and starringCharles Bronsonis released in the US
Tamla/Motown Records releases "Fulfillingness' First Finale," the 17th studio album byStevie Wonder; the album tops both pop and soul charts and includes the hit single "Boogie On Reggae Woman"
Giorgio Armaniand Sergio Galeotti found fashion house Giorgio Armani S.p.A. in Milan, Italy
MLB Cincinnati RedsPete RosepassesFrankie Frischas switch-hit leader with 2,881
Billy Martinresigns as Yankees manager after saying "one is a born liar, the other a convicted one" about Steinbrenner and Jackson

Paramount Pictures releases jukebox musical comedy film "Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band," starring British singers Peter Frampton and theBee Gees, and an all-star cast; featuring late-period songs of the Beatles, it is a critical and commercial flop
Boston Red SoxCarl Yastrzemskihits his 400th home run
US PresidentJimmy CarternamesPaul Volckeras Chairman of the Federal Reserve
Pine Tar Game:George Brett's home run is disallowed against the Yankees (overturned)
"Careless Whisper" single is released byGeorge Michael(credited in the US to Wham!) (Billboard Song of the Year 1985)
Indian Prime MinisterRajiv Gandhisigns a peace accord with Sikh leader Harchand Singh Longowai to settle the three-year Punjab crisis
Fay Vincentreinstates Yankees ownerGeorge Steinbrenner(effective 3/1/93)
"Saving Private Ryan," directed bySteven Spielbergand starringTom Hanks, Edward Burns, andMatt Damon, is released (Academy Award for Best Director 1999)
On the 200th anniversary of his birth, French authorAlexandre Dumas's (The Three Musketeers) ashes are interred in the Panthéon in Paris in a televised ceremony
Ringo Starr's eighth All-Starr Band debuts in concert; members include Colin Hay, Paul Carrack, John Waite, Sheila E., and Mark Rivera
92nd Tour de France: no winner;Lance Armstrongretires after winning a record seventh consecutive victory but disqualified in 2012 for doping
Danielle Steel’s novel “Friends Forever: A Novel” is published
US PresidentBarack Obamabegins a historic 2-day visit to Kenya
Donald Trump’s son-in-law,Jared Kushner, says he did not collude with Russia after meeting with Senate investigators
American singerDemi Lovatois hospitalized after a drug overdose in Los Angeles

19-year-old Hungarian swimmer Kristóf Milák breaksMichael Phelps' 10-year-old 200m butterfly world record at the World Championships in Gwangju, South Korea, swimming 1:50.73, 0.78s faster than Phelps
New UK PMBoris Johnsondrastically reshapes cabinet in his first day in office, appointing Dominic Raab as Foreign Secretary, Priti Patel as Home Secretary and Sajid Javid as Chancellor
Special counsel Robert Mueller reports to the US Senate thatPresident Trumpwas not exonerated of obstruction of justice and that Russia interfered in US election to benefit Trump
"Folklore," the eighth studio album by the American singer-songwriterTaylor Swift, is released via Republic Records
David Ortiz,Gil Hodges, Bud Fowler, Jim Kaat, Minnie Miñoso, and Buck O’Neil are inducted into Baseball Hall of Fame, Cooperstown, NY
Pope Francisarrives in Edmonton, Canada, to begin a penitential trip to meet with and apologize to First Nations, Métis, and Inuit communities for their treatment at church-run residential schools
Singer-songwriter iconJoni Mitchellmakes a surprise concert appearance, joining Brandi Carlile at Newport Folk Festival in Mitchell's first public performance in 19 years
Israeli Prime MinisterBenjamin Netanyahudelivers a defiant address to a joint session of US Congress, defending his country's war in Gaza against Hamas
The long-hated Window Tax, levied based on the number of windows in a dwelling, is abolished in the United Kingdom
WriterO. Henryis released from prison in Austin, Texas, after serving three years for embezzlement from a bank
American explorerHiram Binghamrediscovers Machu Picchu, the Lost City of the Incas
US Vice President Richard Nixon argues with Soviet leaderNikita Khrushchevin Moscow, known as the "Kitchen Debate"
At 12:51 EDT, Apollo 11 returns to Earth after taking the first astronauts to the Moon and returning them safely
Charles Talbot, 1st Duke of Shrewsbury, English politician, helped lead Glorious Revolution to put William III on the throne, born in England (d. 1718)
Louise Ulrike, Queen of Sweden and wife of Adolf Frederik, born in Berlin, Germany (d. 1782)
John Newton, English cleric and hymnist, born in Wapping, London (d. 1807)

Kristian Hellström, Swedish athlete (Olympic bronze 1,500m 1906) and executive (first Secretary-General International Association of Athletics Federations 1913-14), born in Stockholm, Sweden (d. 1946)
